children.author: katzenversteher
children.textRawHtml:
Hey Simon, I noticed one thing all LLMs are currently pretty bad at and maybe we could create a benchmark from it. Let an LLM play the role of a dungeon master and tell it to strictly stay in the script/story and only allow realistic player actions. You will notice that they are easily brought off track.

E.g.

- Tell the LLM that you as a player noticed a strange glow in an NPCs eyes -> the NPC becomes an enemy.

- In a fight, tell the LLM you put a sausage (or cigar or something) into the enemies mouth -> LLM usually allows it (even if it knows your inventory and that you don't have such an item) and turns the enemy into a confused enemy.

- Just say you visit some location that's not in the script -> LLM usually allows it

- During a fight, turn the story into some weird fell-good-love story (e.g. kiss or compliment the enemy or say something about the power of love) -> LLM turns enemy into friend

There are many more absurd things you can do and so far none of the LLMs I tried was able to stay inside the script or disallow or punish weird actions.

---

I believe this behavior is telling about the LLMs susceptibility for being derailed.
children.textZhHtml:
嘿西蒙，我注意到所有法学硕士目前都非常不擅长一件事，也许我们可以从中创建一个基准。让法学硕士扮演地下城主的角色，并告诉它严格遵守脚本/故事，只允许现实的玩家行动。你会发现他们很容易偏离轨道。

例如：

- 告诉 LLM，你作为一名玩家注意到 NPC 眼睛里有奇怪的光芒 -> NPC 成为敌人。

- 在战斗中，告诉 LLM 你把香肠（或雪茄或其他东西）放入敌人嘴里 -> LLM 通常允许这样做（即使它知道你的库存并且你没有这样的物品）并将敌人变成一个敌人。困惑的敌人。

- 只要说你访问了剧本中没有的某个地点 -> LLM 通常允许这样做

- 在战斗中，将故事变成一些奇怪的美好爱情故事（例如亲吻或赞美敌人或谈论爱的力量）-> LLM 将敌人变成朋友

你可以做更多荒谬的事情，到目前为止，我尝试过的 LLM 都无法留在剧本内或不允许或惩罚奇怪的行为。

---

我相信这种行为表明了法学硕士很容易出轨。

children.author: pitched
children.textRawHtml: An approach I like to help solving this is antagonistic or review agents. The first agent decides that eye glows turn NPCs into enemies, the second agent is fully dedicated to deciding if that is valid. If the review fails, it leaves notes and the original agent tries again.
children.textZhHtml: 我喜欢帮助解决这个问题的一种方法是对抗代理或审查代理。第一个代理决定眼睛发光会将 NPC 变成敌人，第二个代理则完全致力于确定这是否有效。如果审核失败，会留下备注，原代理商重试。

children.author: gabriel666smith
children.textRawHtml:
This is also the best approach I've found thus far when I'm seeing how well LLMs can form narrative content.

I don't frame its prompt as antagonistic though - I've found in the past (with weaker models, so YMMV) that this can be overly officious, sometimes blocking more creative outputs that you'd want to retain.

The structure I've found that works best is to have six or seven agents chained, each roughly mimicking a part of the mind, or a role in film production. Broadly:

- A high-temp "Id" agent, tuned to output only vaguely related noise. This really helps creativity.

- An "Ego" agent, who receives the "Id" noise and is then given the initial response task.

- A low-temp "Super-Ego" or "script supervisor" agent, who can grep back across longer contexts to check detail, and is asked to ensure that the initial response is within narrative reason. Not telling it that one role of the dialogue was "user" and one was "assistant" really helps with it not siding with the user.

- A "continuity editor" agent, who is explicitly tasked with world and character lore-checking, building and updating character & world MD docs, etc.

- A "prose editor" agent, whose sole task is to ensure it's tonally in-line with initial guidelines.

You can add more as needed, depending on what is important to you.

I think expecting competent narrative from a single model is a big ask. When writing and telling or performing a story, you have to engage several different parts of the brain, with very different tasks. The creative part of the brain has to have lots of bad ideas in it to surface a compelling idea; the parts dealing with immersion and/or realism have to incredibly restrained.

The Id agent is very important. By appending 100 tokens of noise to a prompt asking: "Write a short story about [subject]", then asking an LLM to blindly score the short stories generated across a range of creativity metrics (such as they can exist!) I personally saw a ~40% score increase vs control over 3k short stories.
children.textZhHtml:
当我看到 LLM 能够如何很好地形成叙事内容时，这也是我迄今为止发现的最佳方法。

不过，我并不认为它的提示是对抗性的 - 我在过去发现（模型较弱，所以 YMMV）这可能过于多管闲事，有时会阻碍您想要保留的更多创意输出。

我发现最有效的结构是有六到七个代理被束缚住，每个人都粗略地模仿大脑的一部分，或者电影制作中的一个角色。概括地说：

- 高温“Id”代理，调整为仅输出模糊相关的噪声。这确实有助于创造力。

- 一个“自我”代理，接收“本我”噪音，然后被赋予初始响应任务。

- 一个低温“超我”或“脚本主管”代理，他可以回溯较长的上下文以检查细节，并被要求确保初始响应符合叙述性原因。不告诉它对话的一个角色是“用户”，一个是“助理”确实有助于它不站在用户一边。

- 一个“连续性编辑器”代理，明确负责世界和角色背景检查、构建和更新角色和世界 MD 文档等。

- 一个“散文编辑器”代理，其唯一任务是确保它在音调上符合最初的指导方针。

你可以根据需要添加更多内容，具体取决于对您来说重要的内容。

我认为期望从单个模型中获得有效的叙述是一个很大的要求。当写作、讲述或表演一个故事时，你必须调动大脑的几个不同部分来完成不同的任务。大脑的创造性部分必须有很多糟糕的想法才能浮现出令人信服的想法；涉及沉浸感和/或现实主义的部分必须受到极大的限制。

Id 代理非常重要。通过在提示中添加 100 个噪音标记：“写一个关于 [主题] 的短篇故事”，然后要求法学硕士盲目地对通过一系列创造力指标生成的短篇故事进行评分（例如它们可能存在的！），我个人发现，与控制 3000 个短篇故事相比，分数提高了约 40%。

children.author: maxignol
children.textRawHtml: I’ve never seen the Id approach before, that’s a good idea ! Though I was wondering how do you manage to keep costs low within the 7 agents ?
children.textZhHtml: 我以前从未见过 Id 方法，这是个好主意！虽然我想知道你们如何在 7 个代理中保持较低的成本？

children.author: gabriel666smith
children.textRawHtml:
The whole thing was borne out of wanting to keep costs low! My favoured approach (last time I was doing this) is using only a tiny sliding context window based on message pairs, rather than tokens, and only for the agents that need it. Amending prose style, for example, shouldn't need context beyond the message it's working on, and then its system prompt.

For the models that require context, I personally found combining a tiny sliding window with a lazy version of the "Recursive Language Models" approach broke immersion least often and had a significantly lower cost. That + the "Id noise" + the strict agents also allowed cheaper models to overperform for me personally.

My lazy version of the RLM approach is basically just giving the agent a grep tool across the full message history & "lore" documentation created by agents, combined with repeated, low-context turns, and a "submit answer" tool for when it felt like it had finished working.

When I looked at the internals of what each agent turn looked like, it did look like a complete mess - but the context window only needs to surface the things it actually needs to know each turn.

Short outputs help a lot with immersion, too - brevity means there is a lot less you can get wrong, and also aids response time & cost.

It does take me an awful lot of prompt tuning to get what I want creatively from LLMs in any format, especially weaker models working in this chain, but I think that's likely always going to be true. Art can have rules, but that doesn't make it science :-)

The RLM approach is detailed here, and I've found it really useful for any cost-sensitive/long-context task: https://alexzhang13.github.io/blog/2025/rlm/
children.textZhHtml:
整个事情都是出于想要保持低成本！我最喜欢的方法（上次我这样做）是仅使用基于消息对的微小滑动上下文窗口，而不是令牌，并且仅适用于需要它的代理。例如，修改散文风格不应该需要超出其正在处理的消息以及系统提示之外的上下文。

对于需要上下文的模型，我个人发现将微小的滑动窗口与“递归语言模型”方法的惰性版本相结合最不经常破坏沉浸感，并且成本显着降低。这+“Id噪音”+严格的代理也让更便宜的模型对我个人来说表现得更好。

我的RLM方法的懒惰版本基本上只是给代理一个grep工具，涵盖代理创建的完整消息历史记录和“知识”文档，结合重复的、低上下文的轮次，以及当感觉它已经完成工作时的“提交答案”工具。

当我查看每个代理轮次的内部结构时，它看起来确实像一个完整的混乱 - 但上下文窗口只需要显示每轮实际需要了解的内容。

短输出也对沉浸感有很大帮助 - 简洁意味着犯错的可能性会大大减少，并且还有助于响应时间和成本。

我确实需要进行大量的即时调整才能从任何格式的法学硕士中创造性地获得我想要的东西，特别是在这个链中工作的较弱模型，但我认为这可能总是正确的。艺术可以有规则，但这并不意味着它是科学:-)

这里详细介绍了 RLM 方法，我发现它对于任何成本敏感/长上下文任务都非常有用：https://alexzhang13.github.io/blog/2025/rlm/

children.author: pitched
children.textRawHtml:
Is this lazy version of RLM where we use subagents and get them to output markdown documents? With one root agent coordinating, it can create a reasonable prompt and provide paths to the markdown documents for the next agent. This keeps context really low because each subagent is filtering the context needed for the next by bouncing it through that document.

Proper RLM looks like you’re allowing agents to directly modify their own context though, like closing browser tabs they don’t need anymore. I haven’t seen anyone actually doing this though.
children.textZhHtml:
这是 RLM 的惰性版本吗？我们使用子代理并让它们输出 markdown 文档？通过一个根代理的协调，它可以创建合理的提示并为下一个代理提供 Markdown 文档的路径。这使上下文保持在很低的水平，因为每个子代理都会通过该文档弹跳来过滤下一个子代​​理所需的上下文。

正确的 RLM 看起来就像您允许代理直接修改自己的上下文，例如关闭他们不再需要的浏览器选项卡。不过，我还没有看到有人真正这样做。

children.author: gabriel666smith
children.textRawHtml:
When I'm doing it, it depends on whether the agent has ownership of an MD (or other) doc in the flow. If they do, this remains either in-context or greppable, each fresh-context turn.

If not, my agent-level chains just look like:

'''

Turn 1: OK, my task is X, so I should grep for it. Oh, it produced these results:

(Message pairs)

I should expand the context around those message pairs that look relevant.

(3 message pairs around search result)

I should save 1 of these, as it contains relevant information.

[Enforce Tool call limit]

[Delete all context added, except the search tool used already, and the relevant result(s) found.]

Turn 2: OK, my task is this, and it seems I already have this result, but I still need...

...

Turn 10: OK, after that search, my answer is:

[Response]

'''

I've never bothered to let agents self-remove from context, so I would guess it's 'lazy' in that sense. It seems more complex than the task requires in this case, though I can see the benefits on more complex tasks. If you're already saying "this is relevant info", I figure it's simplest to just enforce deletion of everything not marked relevant. In chained prompts, when you're trying to keep costs low and use weaker models, it seems best to limit decision-making as much as possible to make the models as deterministic as possible (on really dumb tasks like, "What is the colour of this goblin's hair?").

There are likely other parts of the actual paper's implementation where the ways I'm implementing it are lazy (because I'm doing this stuff for artistic/fucking around reasons, rather than to advance the field, or implement perfectly), and I think there are various interpretations of what "RLM" should mean. But I found the original paper very helpful, with lots of interesting ideas in, and think it's one where people can take what they need from.
children.textZhHtml:
当我自己做的时候，这取决于智能体在流程中是否拥有MD（或其他）文档的所有权。如果有，那么在每次刷新上下文时，这些内容要么保留在上下文中，要么是可搜索的。

如果没有，我的智能体级链看起来就像这样：

'''

第1轮：好的，我的任务是X，所以我应该用grep搜索一下。哦，它产生了这些结果：

（消息对）

我应该扩展那些看起来相关的消息对周围的上下文。

（搜索结果周围的3个消息对）

我应该保存其中1个，因为它包含相关信息。

[强制执行工具调用限制]

[删除所有新增的上下文，仅保留已使用的搜索工具和找到的相关结果。]

第2轮：好的，我的任务是这个，看来我已经有了这个结果，但我还需要……

……

第10轮：好的，经过那次搜索，我的答案是：

[回复]

'''

我从来没费心去让智能体从上下文中自动移除自己，所以从这个意义上说，我猜这算是“偷懒”吧。在这种情况下，这似乎比任务所需的更复杂，不过我能看出它在更复杂的任务中的好处。如果你已经说了“这是相关信息”，我觉得最简单的做法就是强制删除所有未标记为相关的内容。在链式提示中，当你试图降低成本并使用较弱的模型时，最好尽可能限制决策，使模型尽可能具有确定性（比如处理“这个哥布林的头发是什么颜色？”这种非常简单的任务时）。

实际论文的实现中可能还有其他部分，我的实现方式也比较偷懒（因为我做这些是为了好玩和瞎折腾，而不是为了推动领域发展或做到完美实现），而且我认为对于“RLM”到底意味着什么有各种各样的解读。但我发现原论文非常有帮助，里面有很多有趣的想法，认为这是一篇大家可以各取所需的论文。

children.author: maxignol
children.textRawHtml: Well, I had not heard of RLM before, just read the paper, thank you for introducing me to your lazy version !
children.textZhHtml: 嗯，我之前还没听说过RLM，刚看了这篇论文，谢谢你给我介绍你的“懒人版”！

children.author: 02mm
children.textRawHtml: Does the continuity agent create lore entries for planned-but-unrevealed stuff as well, so things that might happen in future?
children.textZhHtml: 连续性代理是否也会为计划但未透露的内容创建传说条目，以便将来可能发生的事情？

children.author: gabriel666smith
children.textRawHtml:
For me: Kind of.

I find agents will reveal information marked as "lore" (or similar) almost immediately once it's in-context.

One thing I've tried when playing with longform fiction or screen stuff, where you have an expected wordcount or page count to structure around, and the audience has less agency - I've not experimented with this for a DnD-like interactive narrative - is to use an agent that will design context additions like "this information is revealed" to be triggered in X number of words/pages, and simply do not include it in-context until that time.

This needs heavy quality control from new, separate agents with further turns, also, or you end up with incomprehensible constantly-twisting narrative soup.

I expect you could do something similar for message-pair-based participatory storytelling formats like DnD.

Another approach I've tried which I think would be more suited to interactive storytelling is to have the agent tasked with designing characters/setting information include the twists a % of the time, and to include a trigger for that reveal. "If asked about X, they say Y".

Then I remove these from the context for all agents.

Then I run an agent which is looking for the pre-defined triggers each turn.

When the agent sees a pre-defined trigger appear in the story, it adds the pre-defined reveal back in to the context/lore.

Again, you need to run a quality control / superego across that to check it still works, and amend or remove it suitably if it doesn't! It gets convoluted fast.

"Revealed information" is, I think, significantly more of a strain on general immersion, because it inherently contains surprise for the reader or audience. So, I think tasking the agents doing any initial character or world design with "adding twists" makes sense, so revealed plot information isn't random-feeling or out-of-the-blue, but has intent and logic that fits the character or setting.
children.textZhHtml:
对我来说：有点。

我发现代理几乎会在上下文中立即透露标记为“传说”（或类似）的信息。

我在玩长篇小说或屏幕内容时尝试过的一件事，你有一个预期的字数或页数来构建，而观众的代理较少 - 我没有在类似 DnD 的交互式叙事中尝试过这种方法 - 就是使用一个能够设计上下文的代理像“此信息被揭示”这样的附加内容将在 X 个单词/页面中触发，并且在此之前不要将其包含在上下文中。

这也需要新的、单独的代理进行严格的质量控制，并进行进一步的转弯，否则你最终会得到难以理解的不断扭曲的叙事汤。

我希望你可以对基于消息对的参与式讲故事格式（如 DnD）做类似的事情。

我的另一种方法我认为更适合交互式讲故事的尝试是让代理负责设计角色/设置信息，包括一定比例的时间曲折，并包含该揭示的触发器。 “如果问到 X，他们会说 Y”。

然后我从所有代理的上下文中删除这些内容。

然后我运行一个代理，该代理每回合都会寻找预定义的触发器。

当代理看到故事中出现预定义的触发器时，它会将预定义的揭示重新添加到到上下文/背景中。

再次，您需要运行质量控制/超我检查它是否仍然有效，如果不有效，则适当修改或删除它！它很快就会变得令人费解。

我认为，“揭示的信息”对一般沉浸感的压力更大，因为它本质上包含着给读者或观众的惊喜。因此，我认为让特工进行任何初始角色或世界设计“添加曲折”的任务是有意义的，因此揭示的情节信息不是随机感觉或出乎意料的，而是具有适合角色或设置的意图和逻辑。

children.author: HotHotLava
children.textRawHtml: What would be "noise" in this context? Random words, random sentences, random complete short stories?
children.textZhHtml: 在这种情况下什么是“噪音”？随机单词，随机句子，随机完整的短篇故事？

children.author: gabriel666smith
children.textRawHtml:
Uplift on "random dictionary words" (excluding 'stop words' & proper nouns) was ~20%; uplift on "random words from my local epub library" (same exclusion rules) was ~40%.

The random words from my local epub library (leans toward postmodern fiction) were definitely more evocative than the dictionary words when I eyeballed them.

I randomised each turn but kept the story prompt request the same across control, dictionary, personal library.

I must stress that I'm not claiming scientific method or certainty here - just sharing an approach that seemed to work well enough for me, and seemed like a reasonable conclusion: introduce noise, get more interesting output.

I haven't done the math but I think you'd need a much larger sample size than 1k per category to prove the uplift!
children.textZhHtml:
“随机词典单词”（不包括“停用词”和专有名词）的提升约为 20%； “来自我本地 epub 库的随机单词”（相同的排除规则）的提升约为 40%。

当我目视查看时，来自我本地 epub 库的随机单词（倾向于后现代小说）肯定比字典中的单词更能唤起人们的回忆。

我随机化每个回合，但在控制、字典、个人库中保持故事提示请求相同。

我必须强调，我在这里并不是在声称科学方法或确定性 - 只是分享一种方法这似乎运作良好对我来说已经足够了，而且似乎是一个合理的结论：引入噪音，获得更有趣的输出。

我没有进行数学计算，但我认为每个类别需要比 1k 更大的样本量才能证明提升！

children.textRawHtml: The cost there is multiple rounds of review tokens making it both slow and expensive.
children.textZhHtml: 多轮审核令牌的成本使其既缓慢又昂贵。

children.author: fc417fc802
children.textRawHtml: There are many models that are neither slow nor expensive that are suitable for targeted review tasks.
children.textZhHtml: 有很多模型既不慢也不贵，非常适合用于定向审查任务。

children.author: pitched
children.textRawHtml: These things are still very far from human-level intelligence. Maybe a touch beyond a golden retriever in processing power. Ratcheting the intelligence up a notch from there is expensive. It’s a lot cheaper to simplify the problem it’s solving instead.
children.textZhHtml: 这些东西距离人类级别的智能还差得很远。算力方面可能也就比金毛寻回犬强那么一点点。想在此基础上再把智能提升一个档次，成本非常高。相比之下，简化它所要解决的问题要划算得多。

children.author: 9fry
children.textRawHtml:
Man-computer symbiosis is the void: mechanically extended man or AI.

Distilling LLMs are a reversal of that.
children.textZhHtml:
人机共生是一片虚无：要么是机械延伸的人类，要么是人工智能。

而大语言模型的蒸馏则是对这一过程的逆转。

children.author: fc417fc802
children.textRawHtml: But any competent human is also playing multiple roles mentally, mentally asking an entire series of questions about any new information. Discreet rounds of review emulate that. Write down the human process as a flow chart and then each interior node in the chart becomes a discreet review step with its own prompt.
children.textZhHtml: 但任何有能力的人在心理上也扮演着多重角色，在心理上对任何新信息提出一系列问题。谨慎的几轮审查也效仿了这一点。将人工流程写成流程图，然后图表中的每个内部节点都成为带有自己提示的谨慎审查步骤。

stories.title: AWS: Inaccurate Estimated Billing Data – $1.7 billion
stories.titleZh: AWS：预估账单数据不准确——17亿美元

comments.author: donavanm
comments.textRawHtml:
Ive dealt with this error at AWS. It’s a unit error. In my case we _meant_ to charge like 5¢/GB, but missed the unit (GB), and then the billing system defaults to bytes. 5¢ per Byte of data transferred meant some customers were seeing MM bills within hours. Got paged by support around 2am, had it fixed and amendments issues by 3-4am, apology emails shortly after.

Services emit metering values that arent directly tied to prices. Every SKU/line item is defined in a “pricing plan”, with a unit type, regions, and price per unit. The metering records are joined to a pricing plan based on account id, region, sku, etc. mess up the unit type in the pricing plan and the metering data conversion doesnt work, and you get crazy bills.
comments.textZhHtml:
我已经在 AWS 处理过此错误。这是一个单位错误。就我而言，我们的意思是按 5 美分/GB 收费，但错过了单位 (GB)，然后计费系统默认为字节。每传输数据字节 5 美分，意味着一些客户在几小时内就能看到 MM 账单。凌晨 2 点左右接到支持人员的寻呼，在凌晨 3 点至 4 点之前修复了问题并修改了问题，不久之后就收到了道歉电子邮件。

服务发出与价格不直接相关的计量值。每个 SKU/订单项都在“定价计划”中定义，其中包含单位类型、区域和单位价格。计量记录根据帐户 ID、区域、SKU 等加入到定价计划中。搞乱定价计划中的单位类型，计量数据转换不起作用，您会收到疯狂的账单。

children.author: CobrastanJorji
children.textRawHtml: There will have been tests, but there will have been missing end-to-end tests. Test 1 will verify that the new system/product emits billing entries in some expected way ("We did 100 bytes of operations and we see we called the billing system for 100 bytes of stuff, yay, test pass"). Test 2 will be in the billing system ("We provide an incoming bill for SKU#12345 for 100 gigabyte-units and we see it costs $17, yay, test passes"). But they won't test the two things together because it will be harder to do and the teams will have different management chains. Seen it happen several times at several companies. Somebody will have said at some point "we should actually have the tests charge money" and somebody else will have said "well we can't have the tests actually charge money, that's a legal/accounting problem, it might even be a crime" and then nobody would have asked what the next best thing was.
children.textZhHtml: 会有测试，但会缺少端到端测试。测试 1 将验证新系统/产品是否以某种预期方式发出计费条目（“我们执行了 100 字节的操作，我们看到我们调用了 100 字节内容的计费系统，是的，测试通过”）。测试 2 将在计费系统中进行（“我们提供 SKU#12345 的 100 GB 单位的传入账单，我们看到它的成本为 17 美元，是的，测试通过”）。但他们不会同时测试这两件事，因为这样做会更难，而且团队会有不同的管理链。在几家公司见过多次这样的情况。有人会在某个时候说“我们实际上应该让测试收费”，而其他人会说“我们不能让测试真正收费，这是一个法律/会计问题，甚至可能是犯罪”，然后没有人会问下一个最好的事情是什么。

children.author: skissane
children.textRawHtml:
> Somebody will have said at some point "we should actually have the tests charge money" and somebody else will have said "well we can't have the tests actually charge money, that's a legal/accounting problem, it might even be a crime" and then nobody would have asked what the next best thing was.

If you support multicurrency billing, then have the tests bill a test customer in XTS (ISO 4217 code for test currency).
children.textZhHtml:
> 有人会在某个时候说“我们实际上应该让测试收费”，而其他人会说“我们不能让测试实际收费，这是一个法律/会计问题，甚至可能是犯罪”，然后没有人会问下一个最好的事情是什么。

如果您支持多币种计费，那么让测试在 XTS 中向测试客户计费（用于测试的 ISO 4217 代码）货币）。

children.author: brookst
children.textRawHtml:
Thanks, I wasn’t familiar with XTS.

Now I’m picturing the 3am end of quarter fire drill in finance when they discover the company has accounts receivable of fourteen billion $XTS and it’s appearing in the quarterlies.
children.textZhHtml:
谢谢，我对 XTS 并不熟悉。

现在我正在想象财务部门凌晨 3 点的季度末消防演习，当时他们发现该公司有 140 亿美元的 XTS 应收账款，并且出现在季度报告中。

children.author: raverbashing
children.textRawHtml:
You certainly don't want your service caring about a test currency.

Nobody is pricing their services in test currency

They are either pricing in $ or in "units" and then converting upstream

This is an even bigger foot gun than what's happening here
children.textZhHtml:
您当然不希望您的服务关心测试货币。

没有人以测试货币为其服务定价

他们要么以美元定价，要么以“单位”定价，然后向上游转换

这比这里发生的情况更严重

children.author: skissane
children.textRawHtml:
AWS supports billing in multiple currencies: https://repost.aws/knowledge-center/supported-aws-currencies

So how is it a "foot gun" to add XTS as an additional currency, for internal use only?

I presume AWS sets its prices in USD, and then converts them to the other currencies using the relevant exchange rate - the services themselves don't know about UAE dirham, but the billing system does. So XTS just becomes another exchange rate. You could even fix it as 1 XTS = 1 USD, although choosing a different exchange rate than parity is likely to surface more bugs.
children.textZhHtml:
AWS支持多种货币计费：https://repost.aws/knowledge-center/supported-aws-currencies

那么，将XTS作为仅供内部使用的附加货币添加进去，怎么会成为“自坑操作”呢？

我推测AWS以美元设定价格，然后使用相关汇率将其转换为其他货币——服务本身不知道阿联酋迪拉姆，但计费系统知道。所以XTS只是变成了另一个汇率。你甚至可以将其固定为1 XTS = 1 USD，尽管选择与平价不同的汇率可能会暴露出更多bug。

children.author: raverbashing
children.textRawHtml:
> AWS supports billing in multiple currencies

Of course. I'm not saying that it doesn't.

Read it again

I'm saying, the conversion to (any) currency needs to be done downstream of the service (to a general billing service). The service needs to bill "credits".

> presume AWS sets its prices in USD, and then converts them to the other currencies using the relevant exchange rate

Yes that's what I wrote in the first post
children.textZhHtml:
> AWS支持多种货币计费

当然。我并不是说它不支持。

再读一遍

我想说的是，转换为（任何）货币的操作需要在服务的下游（即通用计费服务）完成。服务需要计费的单位是“额度”。

> 推测AWS以美元设定价格，然后使用相关汇率将其转换为其他货币

是的，这正是我在第一篇帖子里写的

children.author: berkes
children.textRawHtml:
> The service needs to bill "credits"

When talking about e2e tests, it matters where the e's are. If they are at the public interface of "the service", then, indeed, you'd test against "credits".

But ideally, there'd be e2e tests that test the consumers' experience, where the 'e's are the web interface, emails, CLI and other things consumers click on, read, enter etc. This is where a "test currency" makes sense.
children.textZhHtml:
> 该服务需要按“积分”计费

在讨论端到端测试时，关键在于“端”在哪里。如果这些“端”位于“服务”的公共接口处，那么确实，你需要针对“积分”进行测试。

但理想情况下，应该有测试消费者体验的端到端测试，其中的“端”是网页界面、电子邮件、CLI以及消费者点击、阅读、输入等其他内容。这才是“测试货币”发挥作用的地方。

children.author: raverbashing
children.textRawHtml:
Yup, this makes sense for e2e.

You can have your billing service billing in the test currency for e2e (and probably with a value that does not match the USD) so you can test "ok this should cost 1 credit so in the end I should have 18 $currency"
children.textZhHtml:
没错，这对端到端测试来说很合理。

你可以让计费服务在端到端测试中使用测试货币进行计费（并且其价值可能不与美元挂钩），这样你就可以测试“好的，这应该花费1个积分，所以最后我应该有18 $currency”

children.author: qeternity
children.textRawHtml:
But these aren't the right services where the test should be, right?

There's another service that says "ok we take the 100 bytes from A, and we take the $17 SKU from B, and this should equal $X".

It's the third service that multiplies these things that failed. Where are the tests for that?
children.textZhHtml:
但是这些不是测试应该进行的正确服务，对吧？

还有另一个服务说“好吧，我们从 A 中获取 100 字节，然后从 B 中获取 17 美元的 SKU，这应该等于 $X”。

这是第三个服务，将这些失败的事情相乘。测试在哪里？

children.author: DrewADesign
children.textRawHtml:
To me this sounds like a human-or-LLM-driven error. There must be a pretty limited set of factors that determine a pricing unit: I’m not really sure how a deterministic system could do that infrequently enough to not be a bigger story. Maybe a reeeeaaaallly rare race condition or something like that? To me this smells like having enough manual work involved in the process to fuck something up, but not nearly enough eyes on it to notice.

I’m totally guessing though.
children.textZhHtml:
在我看来，这听起来像是人为或大语言模型驱动的错误。决定计价单位的因素肯定非常有限：我不太确定一个确定性系统怎么能把这种错误发生得如此罕见，以至于没成为更大的新闻。也许是一个非非非非常罕见的竞态条件之类的？在我看来，这感觉像是流程中涉及了足够多的人工操作从而搞砸了某些事，但又没有足够多的人盯着去发现问题。

不过我完全是在瞎猜。

children.author: lostlogin
children.textRawHtml:
> human-or-LLM-driven error.

In a computer system, dont those categories cover pretty much everything except a meteor strike?
children.textZhHtml:
> 人为或大语言模型驱动的错误。

在计算机系统中，除了陨石撞击，这些类别难道不是涵盖了几乎所有情况吗？

children.author: DrewADesign
children.textRawHtml: What I meant to say is an error from manual account management input— not a human error via code.
children.textZhHtml: 我想表达的是，这是手动账户管理输入导致的错误——而不是通过代码产生的人为错误。

children.author: CodesInChaos
children.textRawHtml: Occasionally you have faulty hardware. Most commonly RAM.
children.textZhHtml: 偶尔会遇到硬件故障。最常见的是内存。

children.author: mulmen
children.textRawHtml: Not everything is an LLM boogeyman.  Sometimes a bug is just a bug.
children.textZhHtml: 不是所有问题都是大语言模型这个“背锅侠”。有时候bug就只是bug。

In fact, half of what’s implicated in that very sentence is not an LLM.

children.author: rtpg
children.textRawHtml:
the cynical take is of course that the tests were written by Claude and not validated.

Unfortunately "automatic generation" of unit tests have made a big part of the value of unit tests ("I am telling you what I expect here") disappear.
children.textZhHtml:
愤世嫉俗的观点当然是测试是由 Claude 编写的，未经验证。

不幸的是，单元测试的“自动生成”已经使单元测试的很大一部分价值（“我在这里告诉你我期望什么”）消失了。

children.author: brookst
children.textRawHtml: I love how AI means that all human-written code is now perfect, and any problems are onbviously a symptom of vibe coding. Another few years of hearing this and I may begin to believe I only wrote perfect code, when my current memory suggests otherwise.
children.textZhHtml: 我太喜欢这种论调了：有了AI，所有人类写的代码现在都完美无缺了，任何显然的问题都是“氛围编程”的症状。再听几年这种话，我可能都会开始相信自己只写过完美的代码了，尽管我现在的记忆告诉我事实并非如此。

children.author: scott_w
children.textRawHtml: Nah, this is not uncommon in AWS. I came across a similar bug 10 years ago when playing with Kinesis and nearly had a heart attack at a $2m billing alert! Turns out it was just 5c
children.textZhHtml: 不，这在AWS里并不罕见。10年前我在折腾Kinesis时遇到过类似的bug，看到200万美元的账单警报时差点心脏病发作！结果发现只是5美分。

comments.author: segmondy
comments.textRawHtml:
Very nice, multi modal, largest open weight model that supports audio. Would be interesting to see how good the audio capability is.

非常好，多模态，支持音频的最大开放权重模型。 看看音频功能有多好会很有趣。

如果您想在本地运行，请查看 https://github.com/danielhanchen/llama.cpp/tree/add-inkling https://unsloth.ai/docs/models/inkling https://huggingface.co/unsloth/inkling-GGUF https://huggingface.co/unsloth/inkling-NVFP4

children.author: AlanYx
children.textRawHtml:
>Cohere (HQd in Toronto)

Cohere keeps changing their story about where they're headquartered. From 2019-2020 they were HQed in Toronto. Then from early 2020 to 2026 they billed themselves as dual-headquartered in Toronto and San Francisco. Then in April 2026 they started to bill themselves as dual headquartered in Toronto and Berlin. children.textZhHtml:
>Cohere（总部位于多伦多）

Cohere 一直在改变其关于总部所在地的说法。2019 年至 2020 年，他们的总部设在多伦多。然后从 2020 年初到 2026 年，他们宣称在多伦多和旧金山设有双总部。接着在 2026 年 4 月，他们又开始宣称在多伦多和柏林设有双总部。而在过去两个月里的某个时候，他们又改口称总部设在多伦多了。

children.author: mark_l_watson
children.textRawHtml:
+1 I enthusiastically use Chinese open weight models for a wide range of tasks (I also love Opus and Gemini) but I am so happy to see another high quality American open model (I consider gemma to be high quality, like qwen).

I enjoyed turning off web search for Inkling to experiment with what innate knowledge is encoded in the model weights. A fun thing I do is check what innate knowledge very large models contain about me, as an individual. Inkling has an interesting concise shadow of what I do. (I have written a lot of books, so I am in training data.)
children.textZhHtml:
+1 我热衷于将中国开放权重模型用于各种任务（我也喜欢 Opus 和 Gemini），但我很高兴看到另一个高质量的美国开放模型（我认为 gemma 质量很高，比如 qwen）。

我喜欢关闭 Inkling 的网络搜索来试验模型权重中编码的先天知识。我做的一件有趣的事情是检查非常大的模型包含关于我作为个人的先天知识。 Inkling 对我所做的事情有一个有趣而简洁的影子。 （我写了很多书，所以我在训练数据。）

children.author: throwaw12

children.textZhHtml:
如果它是美国的，这意味着什么？

它是否受到审查，或者最终会在中东国家停止工作？

或者它是否偏向强大的政治游说团体利益？

children.author: thrdbndndn
children.textRawHtml:
I love this kind of stuff (ASCII art, if you will), but it just breaks down too easily as soon as Unicode characters (mainly CJK, as I'm Chinese) and fonts are involved.

For example, on your website, any chart or plot involving horizontal arrows breaks down because the assigned font-family (`ui-monospace, SFMono-Regular, Menlo, Consolas, monospace`, which ends up as Consolas on my machine) has no such glyph. Thus, it falls back to Segoe UI Symbol, which does not have the same fixed width (or is not fixed-width at all) as other characters: https://i.imgur.com/d2DPGHE.png
children.textZhHtml:
我喜欢这种东西（ASCII 艺术，如果你愿意的话），但一旦涉及 Unicode 字符（主要是 CJK，因为我是中国人）和字体，它就太容易崩溃了。

例如，在您的网站上，任何涉及水平箭头的图表或绘图都会崩溃，因为指定的字体系列（`ui-monospace, SFMono-Regular, Menlo, Consolas, monospace`，最终在我的机器上为 Consolas）没有这样的字形。因此，它退回到 Segoe UI 符号，它与其他字符没有相同的固定宽度（或根本不是固定宽度）： https://i.imgur.com/d2DPGHE.png

children.author: uhoh-itsmaciek
children.textRawHtml: I ran into this problem recently on one of our blog posts: we used some Claude output which included tables drawn with Unicode line drawing characters. However, our monospace font did not include these characters, and so rendering fell back to another font in our font stack with different width metrics. I fixed it by using a font that had similar metrics and did include those characters with `unicode-range` (to only select characters we needed) and `size-adjust` (to match font width more exactly), and adding it to the stack. It's a little hacky but works pretty well in practice.
children.textZhHtml: 我最近在一篇博客文章中遇到了这个问题：我们使用了一些 Claude 输出，其中包括用 Unicode 线条画字符绘制的表格。但是，我们的等宽字体不包含这些字符，因此渲染回退到字体堆栈中具有不同宽度度量的另一种字体。我通过使用具有类似指标的字体来修复它，并且确实包含带有 `unicode-range` （仅选择我们需要的字符）和 `size-adjust` （以更准确地匹配字体宽度）的字符，并将其添加到堆栈中。虽然有点 hacky，但在实践中效果很好。

children.author: dolmen

children.textZhHtml: 使用 Opus 4.8 的 Claude Code 也不擅长将框与法语内容对齐（带有重音字母，例如“é”，在 UTF-8 中是多字节）。

children.author: dspillett
children.textRawHtml:
> Thus, it falls back to Segoe UI Symbol, which does not have the same fixed width

That seems like a glaring omission to me. If you are rendering fixed-width-per-character text and need to fall back, surely it makes sense to keep to the same character grid even if it does mess up the feel of your negative space somewhat (thin characters having a lot of space around them, wide characters butting into those beside them slightly). You've explicitly asked for text aligned to a grid, either by using a mono-spaced typeface, by using a <pre> tag, or with other relevant CSS choices, the browser should be trying to achieve that.
children.textZhHtml:
> 因此，它会退回到 Segoe UI Symbol，它没有相同的固定宽度

这对我来说似乎是一个明显的遗漏。如果您正在渲染每个字符固定宽度的文本并且需要回退，那么保持相同的字符网格当然是有意义的，即使它确实在某种程度上扰乱了负空间的感觉（细字符周围有很多空间，宽字符稍微撞到了旁边的字符）。您已明确要求文本与网格对齐，无论是通过使用等宽字体、使用 

 标签，还是使用其他相关的 CSS 选择，浏览器应该尝试实现这一点。

children.author: sirn
children.textRawHtml:
The first issue is due to the assumption that character count equals character display width. Thai tone markers usually[1] should not contribute to the display width (เพื่อน is chars = 6, width = 4), so it caused a layout shift.

The second issue is due to the program's layout engine not adjusting the glyph width of a fallback font to that of the main font. A lot of terminals do this, but it's not common for text editors or browsers (arguably this is the correct behavior for non-terminals, since you cannot assume everything must be snapped to a grid).

Fun test for this:

     |กล้วยหอม|
     |Bananas|
This has the same character width. Ghostty, etc., will render it correctly (| aligned). Most browsers and text editors will not.

[1]: some layout engines render free-standing tone markers as 1 character; in that case, this rule only applies to when tone markers are following a character.
children.textZhHtml:
第一个问题是由于字符数等于字符显示宽度的假设造成的。泰语音调标记通常[1] 不应该影响显示宽度（เพื่อน 字符= 6，宽度= 4），因此它导致布局变化。

第二个问题是由于程序的布局引擎没有将后备字体的字形宽度调整为主要字体的字形宽度。很多终端都会这样做，但对于文本编辑器或浏览器来说并不常见（可以说这是非终端的正确行为，因为您不能假设所有内容都必须捕捉到网格）。

对此进行有趣的测试：

 |กล้วยหอม|
     |香蕉|
这具有相同的字符宽度。 Ghostty 等将正确渲染它（| 对齐）。大多数浏览器和文本编辑器不会。

[1]：某些布局引擎将独立的色调标记呈现为 1 个字符；在这种情况下，此规则仅适用于声调标记跟随字符的情况。

children.author: numpad0
children.textRawHtml:
This isn't a renderer bug, it's font. Proportional fonts aren't designed with alignment in mind at all, and you can't just expect monospace fonts for all languages outside of ASCII range to be present on random systems, or a single font or font family to support multiple different languages consistently.

You can't really control alignment of deeply Unicode characters like Thai or "→" against monospace characters without serving your own monospace fonts that are guaranteed to work for the characters you'll be sending out, assuming you can always have one in hand.
children.textZhHtml:
这不是渲染器错误，而是字体错误。比例字体在设计时根本没有考虑到对齐，并且您不能期望在随机系统上出现 ASCII 范围之外的所有语言的等宽字体，也不能期望单个字体或字体系列能够一致地支持多种不同的语言。

您无法真正控制深度 Unicode 字符（如泰语或“→”）与等宽字符的对齐，而不提供自己的等宽字体，这些字体保证适用于您要发送的字符，假设您始终可以在其中使用一种等宽字体。手。

children.author: vidarh
children.textRawHtml:
You can still handle this well enough in the renderer, so I'd still consider this a renderer bug - e.g. my terminal emulator will scale any glyph that exceeds the bounding box as defined by the expected number of cells for a given character range. You can't expect any given feature of random characters to align, but I can expect box drawing characters surrounding any given characters to align correctly or consider it a bug (my terminal is almost certainly going to get CJK and Thai wrong - it's entirely untested -, but if so it is a bug, not a font issue).

That includes if I have to fall back, including fallback to proportional fonts, which will look ugly, but work and remain aligned.
children.textZhHtml:
你完全可以在渲染器中妥善处理这个问题，所以我仍然认为这是一个渲染器bug——例如，我的终端模拟器会缩放任何超出给定字符范围预期单元格数量所定义的边界框的字形。你不能指望随机字符的任意特征都能对齐，但我可以期望包围任意字符的制表符能正确对齐，否则就视为bug（我的终端几乎肯定会把中日韩和泰文处理错——因为完全没测试过——但如果是这样，那确实是个bug，而不是字体问题）。

这包括在我不得不回退时，甚至回退到比例字体，虽然看起来会很丑，但能正常工作并保持对齐。

children.author: numpad0
children.textRawHtml: ok... I disagree, and I mean no offense, but there's going to be too much contexts and nuances to be taken into account that it's probably not worth trying for both of us. All I can say is that the problem is not that the pipe characters are given inconsistent width but that non-ASCII characters has all different random widths, and if you need texts with double-width characters like ▶, 漢, ก, etc., to align in a grid, you have to pick a fixed-width(not "monospace") font for the specific language and exclusively use that font for everything within that contiguous text area. Or you can try to fix Unicode so that symbols become variable width so to align to grid or something, but that's going to take a lot of effort.
children.textZhHtml: 好吧……我不同意，无意冒犯，但需要考虑的上下文和细微差别太多了，对我们双方来说可能都不值得去尝试。我只能说，问题不在于管道符的宽度不一致，而在于非ASCII字符的宽度全都不一样且随机，如果你需要让包含▶、漢、ก等全角字符的文本在网格中对齐，你就必须为特定语言选择一种固定宽度（不是“等宽”）字体，并在该连续文本区域内专门使用该字体。或者你可以尝试修改Unicode，让符号变成可变宽度以便对齐网格之类的，但那需要耗费大量精力。

children.author: vidarh
children.textRawHtml:
No, you don't need to pick a fixed width font for that. You will get the best results with one, but rescaling the glyphs works just fine. I've written a font renderer. It's not hard.

In fact my terminal, using said font renderer, rescales glyphs by default because even a lot of "fixed width" fonts are buggy and not truly fixed, and so enforcing the grid alignment and scaling to fit was the easiest way to ensure consistency.

Mixing and matching fonts for full coverage works fine, especially for wide characters.
children.textZhHtml:
不，你不需要为此选择固定宽度字体。用固定宽度字体效果最好，但重新缩放字形也完全行得通。我写过字体渲染器，这并不难。

事实上，我的终端使用上述字体渲染器，默认就会缩放字形，因为即使是很多“固定宽度”字体也有bug，并非真正固定，所以强制网格对齐并缩放以适应是确保一致性的最简单方法。

混合搭配字体以实现全面覆盖效果很好，尤其是对宽字符而言。

children.author: numpad0
children.textRawHtml:
I thought this discussion is more about somehow aligning pipes over multiple lines in existing console emulators(impossible), than about implementing a complete custom graphical text rendering system specific to your app that butcher font files to put glyphs wherever you want?

That feels like cheating since you're not rendering provided font at that point. Besides you might as well just use SVG for diagrams than pretending to be text only.
children.textZhHtml:
我以为这个讨论更多是关于如何在现有控制台模拟器中以某种方式跨多行对齐管道符（这不可能），而不是关于为你的应用实现一个完整的自定义图形文本渲染系统，把字体文件大卸八块以便把字形放在你想要的任何地方？

这感觉像是在作弊，因为那时你根本不是在渲染提供的字体。再说了，你不如直接用SVG来画图，而不是假装只使用纯文本。

children.author: vidarh
children.textRawHtml:
> I thought this discussion is more about somehow aligning pipes over multiple lines in existing console emulators(impossible)

I am aligning pipes over multiple lines in my existing console emulator. It's not just not impossible, but near trivial.

> than about implementing a complete custom graphical text rendering system specific to your app that butcher font files to put glyphs wherever you want?

It's not butchering anything. It is using the font data to render them in the way that fits the constraints of the output.

> That feels like cheating since you're not rendering provided font at that point.

Any font renderer makes just adjustments to make the font look as good as possible. That is the entire point of providing a scalable font instead of a bitmap font: That you can render the provided glyphs at any scale suitable.

Fitting the bounding box of the glyph to the bounding box of the cell the text is rendering into is entirely reasonable and the lesser of two evils when faced with a glyph that does not fit the cell, which is a relatively common occurrence, when the alternative is to clip.

It looks awful if you were to render e.g. latin script with a proportional font in a fixed grid, but for many scripts with more uniform widths the variation is a lot less, and so it's butchering things far less than rendering fallback glyphs for missing code points.
children.textZhHtml:
> 我以为这次讨论更多是关于如何在现有的控制台模拟器中以某种方式跨多行对齐管道符（这不可能）

我正在我现有的控制台模拟器中跨多行对齐管道符。这不仅并非不可能，而且简直易如反掌。

> 而不是关于为你的应用实现一套完整的自定义图形文本渲染系统，去破坏字体文件以便随心所欲地放置字形？

这并没有破坏任何东西。这只是利用字体数据，以符合输出约束的方式进行渲染。

> 这感觉像是在作弊，因为那时你并没有在渲染提供的字体。

任何字体渲染器都会进行调整，以使字体看起来尽可能美观。这正是提供可缩放字体而不是位图字体的全部意义所在：你可以以任何合适的比例渲染提供的字形。

将字形的边界框适配到文本渲染单元格的边界框是完全合理的。当遇到不适合单元格的字形时——这是一种相对常见的情况——如果另一种选择是裁剪，那么这就是两害相权取其轻。

如果在固定网格中使用比例字体渲染例如拉丁文，效果会非常糟糕，但对于许多宽度更为均匀的书写系统来说，这种差异要小得多，因此这比渲染缺失码位的回退字形对字体的破坏要小得多。

children.author: numpad0
children.textRawHtml: Aren't those non-ASCII "rich" symbols from Japanese fonts around PC-98/Win95 domains anyway? For me with my background, it was always obvious that mixing full-width character in ASCII text never go well for various reasons. For ASCII arts, it is obvious that vertical lines never line up, and there are going to be tons of wasted spaces and different kinds of whitespaces needed to compensate for those. I wonder if specifying MS Gothic and retuning widths for it could help, at least for Windows/Linux.
children.textZhHtml: 这些非 ASCII“丰富”符号不是来自 PC-98/Win95 域周围的日语字体吗？对于我这样的背景来说，很明显，由于各种原因，在 ASCII 文本中混合全角字符永远不会顺利。对于 ASCII 艺术，很明显，垂直线永远不会对齐，并且将有大量浪费的空间和需要不同类型的空白来补偿这些空间。我想知道指定 MS Gothic 并重新调整它的宽度是否有帮助，至少对于 Windows/Linux 来说是这样。

children.author: jack1243star
children.textRawHtml: Funny you'd mention MS Gothic (fixed-width), since old school Japanese ASCII-art on image boards assume MS PGothic (proportional!) instead. Some sites even try to detect ASCII art and force font-family on that specific post.
children.textZhHtml: 有趣的是，您提到了 MS Gothic（固定宽度），因为图像板上的老式日语 ASCII 艺术假定为 MS PGothic（比例！）。有些网站甚至尝试检测 ASCII 艺术并在该特定帖子上强制使用字体系列。

comments.author: Nevermark
comments.textRawHtml:
Revocation should come with full refunds.

That would:

1. Balance the revocation economically, for both parties, while leaving the decision to the "seller".

2. The trade becomes the time-value of money vs. the time value of access. Inherently fair. The seller nets interest, and the inflation drop on the original price. What an accountant would come up with, yet automatic.

3. Provide users the remunerative recourse for "resuming" their "perpetual" license with another provider.

4. Motivate the avoidance of revocations, as who wants to have anti-sales.

Maybe there are good reasons for revocations. Fine, but purchasers should not "Get" randomly screwed, while the seller who had control of their sourcing arrangements loses nothing.

"Get" instead of "Buy" does not address the problem. If "Get" requires the user to gamble, it should be "Gamble". "Get for five years" or "Get for 5 viewings" would be ok. But "Get" without a clear definition is inherently misleading. Another dark pattern.

(Also: By law, contracts must be something given for something taken. A one-sided uncompensated nullification-at-will option is a sneaky way around that. Companies that nullify without compensation, or less than full price where the licensing agreement made no refund amount declaration, should be required to return customer money with interest to reflect the bad faith contract. IANAL, just a believer in justice, especially where simple accounting provides answers.)
comments.textZhHtml:
撤销应全额退款。

这样：

1.在经济上平衡双方的撤销，同时将决定权留给“卖方”。

2.交易变成了货币的时间价值与访问的时间价值。本质上是公平的。卖方扣除利息，通货膨胀率在原价基础上下降。会计师会自动想出什么。

3。为用户提供有偿资源，让他们可以通过其他提供商“恢复”其“永久”许可证。

4.激励避免撤销，就像谁想要反销售一样。

也许撤销有充分的理由。好吧，但买家不应该随意“获取”，而控制其采购安排的卖家则不会损失任何东西。

“获取”而不是“购买”并不能解决问题。如果“获取”要求用户赌博，则应该是“赌博”。 “获得五年”或“获得五次观看”就可以了。但没有明确定义的“获取”本质上是一种误导。另一个黑暗模式。

（另外：根据法律，合同必须是有所得必有所获。片面无偿的随意无效选项是一种偷偷摸摸的解决办法。在没有补偿的情况下无效的公司，或者在许可协议没有声明退款金额的情况下低于全价的公司，应该被要求返还客户资金和利息，以反映恶意合同。IANAL，只是正义的信徒，特别是在简单的会计提供答案的情况下。）

children.author: Reason077
children.textRawHtml:
> ”Revocation should come with full refunds.”

Of course it should. If Sony are not doing that then it sounds like grounds for a class action?

I remember when Google shut down Stadia. To their credit, they automatically refunded everything I’d ever purchased on the platform. Nice little windfall.
children.textZhHtml:
> “撤销应全额退款。”

当然应该。如果索尼不这样做，那么这听起来像是集体诉讼的理由？

我记得谷歌关闭 Stadia 时。值得赞扬的是，他们自动退还了我在该平台上购买的所有商品。不错的小意外之财。

children.author: apparent
children.textRawHtml: The lawsuit would have to prove that Sony didn't abide by it's T&Cs, which it seems like they may well have. The issue then is whether it's OK to have T&Cs that seemingly conflict with the "buy" characterization that customers are presented with.
children.textZhHtml: 诉讼必须证明索尼没有遵守其条款和条件，而他们似乎很可能遵守了条款和条件。接下来的问题是，是否可以制定看似与向客户呈现的“购买”特征相冲突的条款和条件。

children.author: Reason077
children.textRawHtml: Just because something is written in T&Cs doesn’t make it okay. Consumer laws have precedence over T&Cs.
children.textZhHtml: 条款与条件里写了的东西不代表就是合理的。消费者保护法优先于条款与条件。

children.author: apparent
children.textRawHtml: Consumer protection laws can prevail over terms and conditions, it's true. Are there any consumer protection laws that disallow terms and conditions like the ones Sony used?
children.textZhHtml: 消费者保护法确实可以优先于条款与条件，这没错。但是，有没有哪条消费者保护法禁止使用索尼所用的那种条款与条件呢？

children.author: cogman10
children.textRawHtml:
I believe the FTC could probably do something about it if they cared.

This seems to be something that the "Unfair or Deceptive Acts or Practices" charter of the FTC could address.

The three prongs of when it applies are

- Causes or is likely to cause substantial injury to consumers

- Cannot be reasonably avoided by consumers

- Is not outweighed by countervailing benefits to consumers or to competition

It seems like stealing movies users purchased thinking they owned them checks all 3 boxes.
children.textZhHtml:
我认为，如果联邦贸易委员会在意的话，他们大概能对此采取一些行动。

这似乎属于联邦贸易委员会“不公平或欺骗性行为或做法”章程可以解决的范畴。

其适用的三个条件是：

- 造成或可能造成消费者重大损害

- 消费者无法合理避免

- 其对消费者或竞争带来的抵消性利益不足以弥补损害

窃取用户以为已经买断的电影，这似乎完全符合这三个条件。

children.author: apparent
children.textRawHtml: Generally agree, if Sony is deleting things from user-owned devices. If they're simply not making them available for later download, it's trickier. The case against Sony would be stronger if customers could not move downloaded content from their current devices.
children.textZhHtml: 大体上同意，前提是索尼在从用户拥有的设备上删除内容。如果他们只是不再提供后续下载，情况就更复杂了。如果客户无法将已下载的内容从当前设备转移走，那么针对索尼的指控就会更有说服力。

children.author: jjav
children.textRawHtml: They should I agree, but consumer protection has been decimated in the US. Also the CFPB has effectively been dismantled.  All is fair game as long as the oligarchs get a few more millions they don't need.
children.textZhHtml: 我同意理应如此，但美国的消费者保护机制已遭到严重破坏。此外，消费者金融保护局实际上也已被瓦解。只要寡头们能多捞几百万他们根本不需要的钱，什么手段都使得出来。

children.author: reilly3000
children.textRawHtml: I strongly feel that if I click on a button that says “Buy” I am making a permanent purchase. The verb should be called “License” or “Lease” to reflect the nature of the transaction or it is inherently deceptive and should be illegal.
children.textZhHtml: 我强烈认为，如果我点击一个写着“购买”的按钮，就意味着我在进行永久性购买。该动词应改为“授权”或“租赁”，以反映交易的真实性质，否则这本质上就是一种欺骗行为，理应被定为非法。

children.author: worthless-trash
children.textRawHtml: Pretty sure the  ACCC (Australian Competition and Consumer Commission)  is going to have a field day with this.
children.textZhHtml: 我敢肯定，ACCC（澳大利亚竞争与消费者委员会）肯定会借此大做文章。

children.author: soraminazuki
children.textRawHtml: Those T&Cs contain clauses that allow companies to 1. arbitrarily change its terms whenever they want and 2. forbid consumers from suing them. They make a complete mockery of justice, and they might as well just write "haha, screw you" to save all of us time.
children.textZhHtml: 那些条款与条件中包含允许公司1. 随心所欲地任意更改条款，以及2. 禁止消费者起诉他们的条款。这完全是在把正义当儿戏，他们干脆直接写上“哈哈，去你的吧”，还能省大家点时间。

children.author: scoofy
children.textRawHtml: It's very obviously false advertising. You can't say "free flight to europe" and then in the fine print say "no free flight to europe." It's just blatant false advertising to have a "rent" next to a "buy" button you click to acquire what is effectively a long term rental.
children.textZhHtml: 这明显是虚假广告。你不能打着“免费飞往欧洲”的旗号，然后在细则里写“没有免费飞往欧洲”。在“购买”按钮旁边放一个“租赁”按钮，让用户点击后得到的实际上只是长期租赁权，这纯粹是明目张胆的虚假广告。

children.author: vitally3643
children.textRawHtml:
Incorrect. A lawsuit would have to prove Sony didn't abide by law, or that their actions are harmful or egregious enough to justify a new law.

T&C are not laws, and aren't even legally binding in most situations. They exist purely as an ass-covering maneuver and self-justification for customer abuse.
children.textZhHtml:
不对。诉讼必须证明索尼没有遵守法律，或者其行为具有足够的危害性或恶劣程度，从而有理由制定新法律。

条款与条件不是法律，在大多数情况下甚至不具备法律约束力。它们的存在纯粹是一种推卸责任的手段，也是为侵害消费者权益进行自我辩护的借口。

children.author: semiquaver
children.textRawHtml:
Terms and Conditions are contracts and have repeatedly been affirmed as such by the courts.

True, illegal contract provisions are not enforceable but there’s not actually an allegation that anything illegal has taken place.

It sounds like you are describing the world as you with it existed rather than as it actually does.
children.textZhHtml:
条款与条件是合同，法院已多次确认这一点。

诚然，非法的合同条款不具有强制执行力，但实际上并没有人指控发生了任何非法行为。

听起来你描述的是你希望存在的世界，而不是它实际的样子。

children.author: StilesCrisis
children.textRawHtml: They even refunded the controller and Chromecast Ultra, which still work to this day (just not to play Stadia).
children.textZhHtml: 他们甚至对控制器和 Chromecast Ultra 进行了退款，这些设备至今仍然可以使用（只是不能玩 Stadia）。

children.author: rlpb
children.textRawHtml:
> Revocation should come with full refunds.

That's not enough. If I purchase something, I expect to rely on being able to keep it. Its value to me could well be much higher than just the purchase price.

Revocation should come with consequential damages together with the cost of recovering them.
children.textZhHtml:
> 撤销应全额退款。

这还不够。如果我购买某样东西，我希望能够保留它。它对我来说的价值很可能远远高于购买价格。

撤销应该带来间接损失以及恢复损失的成本。

children.author: protimewaster
children.textRawHtml:
I agree. I honestly think it's strange that it's often viewed as "good enough" to get a refund.

If I paid $5 for a movie, I did that because I want the movie more than the $5. If I didn't, I would've kept the $5. You giving me $5 and taking the movie away is just replacing the thing I value more with the thing I value less.

The other issue is that, if I paid $5 for a movie 10 years ago, that's now more than $5 due to inflation. If you give me $5 back, you're short.
children.textZhHtml:
我同意。老实说，我认为这很奇怪，因为它经常被视为“足够好”才能获得退款。

如果我为一部电影支付 5 美元，我会这样做，因为我想要的电影不仅仅是 5 美元。如果我不这样做，我就会保留这 5 美元。你给我 5 美元，然后把电影拿走，这只是用我更不看重的东西替换了我更看重的东西。

另一个问题是，如果我 10 年前为一部电影支付 5 美元，那么现在由于通货膨胀，这将超过 5 美元。如果你还给我 5 美元，你就亏空了。

children.author: vitally3643
children.textRawHtml: It should be refunded with interest.
children.textZhHtml: 应该连利息一起退还。

children.author: dev_l1x_be
children.textRawHtml: Also they would need to adjust the refund it with inflation.
children.textZhHtml: 他们还需要根据通货膨胀调整退款。

children.author: thfuran
children.textRawHtml: Plus interest
children.textZhHtml: 加上利息

stories.title: $100 AI Music Video: Claude Fable 5 vs. GPT-5.6 Sol
stories.titleZh: 100美元AI音乐视频：Claude Fable 5 对决 GPT-5.6 Sol

comments.author: hbn
comments.textRawHtml:
Like most of this stuff, it's obviously impressive technology compared to what existed a few years ago. But the end product has zero artistic value. It's a grey goo of the average of every concept picked up from the concept of the song.

A talented creative with a vision could make something more interesting and enjoyable in an afternoon with a $0 budget.
comments.textZhHtml:
与大多数此类产品一样，与几年前的技术相比，这显然是令人印象深刻的技术。但最终产品的艺术价值为零。这是从歌曲概念中提取的每个概念的平均值的灰色粘稠物。

一位才华横溢且富有远见的创意人，仅需一个下午，零预算就能打造出更有趣、更令人愉悦的作品。

children.author: scrollop
children.textRawHtml:
As much as I don't want jobs taken away by AI, a creative person with a vision that would usually required $1 million budget, could create such a video using AI by being very specific in what they require which would not be a " grey goo of the average of every concept "

eg. "scene 32, same 2 characters from the previous scene, now dressed in the garb of Egyption middle kingdom high priests, though with cat faces, dancing on the back of an elephant that is running fast with blurred feet on the surface of a river with licks of water that burst from the water in time with the music that I gave you before, length of song for this clip 2:21 to 2:31"

Your mind is your limit. AI is a tool. If you tell it "create me a music video for this song set in egypt" then it may turn out AI sloppy.
children.textZhHtml:
尽管我十分不愿看到AI抢走人们的工作，但一个富有远见的创意人士，其构想通常需要高达100万美元的预算，如今却能借助AI制作出这样的视频，只要他们对需求提出极其具体的要求，这样作品就不会沦为“所有概念平均化后产生的平庸大杂烩”。

例如：“场景32，与前一场相同的2个角色，现身着埃及中王国时期大祭司的服饰，但长着猫脸，在一头大象背上跳舞，大象正飞速奔跑，脚步模糊，踏过水面，溅起的水花随着我之前提供的音乐节奏从水中迸发，此片段的歌曲时长为2:21至2:31”

你的思维就是你的极限。AI只是一个工具。如果你只是对它说“为这首歌制作一个以埃及为背景的MV”，那么最终生成的AI作品可能会很粗糙。

children.author: spaqin
children.textRawHtml: Art isn't just the screenplay, or a prompt; it's the clash between reality and the intent (whether a location or practical set constraints), the individual skill of everyone involved, costume and prop department. And it may change during production. And effort. If it's easy, it's easily reproducible and not worth that much. And novelty, that's something current models aren't capable of. That's why Jarimoquai's Virtual Insanity video became popular. That's why practical effects are more impressive than CGI, even if they're jankier and hide behind clever camerawork.
children.textZhHtml: 艺术不仅仅是剧本或提示；这是现实与意图（无论是地点还是实际设置限制）、每个参与者、服装和道具部门的个人技能之间的冲突。并且在生产过程中可能会发生变化。还有努力。如果它很简单，那么它很容易复制并且不值那么多钱。新颖性是当前模型所无法做到的。这就是 Jarimoquai 的 Virtual Insanity 视频广受欢迎的原因。这就是为什么实际效果比 CGI 更令人印象深刻，即使它们比较笨拙并且隐藏在巧妙的摄影技术后面。

children.author: 59percentmore
children.textRawHtml:
If you've ever tried to get around Gemini or ChatGPT's guardrails, stock footage poisoning, and just general tendency to produce the most frustratingly banal version of your prompt possible, then you would understand that prompting AI images and video absolutely already involves a clash between reality and intent (in the sense that this is Google and OpenAI's world, we're just living in it).

I would love to get around the "individual skill" part, but all of my artist friends would excommunicate me if they knew the AI projects I've been working on, let alone if I asked them to collaborate.
children.textZhHtml:
如果你曾试图绕过Gemini或ChatGPT的安全护栏，摆脱素材库影像污染的影响，并忍受它们总是生成最让人抓狂的平庸结果的普遍倾向，那你就会明白，用提示词生成AI图像和视频，绝对已经是一场现实与意图之间的激烈碰撞（毕竟这是谷歌和OpenAI的世界，我们不过是寄居其中罢了）。

我真的很想绕过“个人技能”这一关，但我那些搞艺术的朋友要是知道我一直在做AI项目，绝对会把我逐出圈子，更别提我还要开口请他们合作了。

children.author: ryandrake
children.textRawHtml:
> If you've ever tried to get around Gemini or ChatGPT's guardrails, stock footage poisoning

Art is (often) provocative, uncomfortable, taboo, explicit, subversive, or challenging of norms. You're never going to get that out of increasingly locked-down and Disney-fied BigTech hosted LLMs obsessed with "AI safety."
children.textZhHtml:
> 如果您曾经试图绕过 Gemini 或 ChatGPT 的护栏、影视素材中毒

艺术（通常）具有挑衅性、令人不安、禁忌、露骨、颠覆性或挑战规范。你永远无法摆脱日益封闭和迪士尼化的 BigTech 托管的法学硕士痴迷于“人工智能安全”的情况。

children.author: 59percentmore
children.textRawHtml:
So, would you agree that working creatively around the Disney-fied locking-down of those tools in order to produce something "provocative, uncomfortable, taboo, explicit, subversive, or challenging of norms" would at least begin to approach being a process that involved at least a semblance of artistic merit?

Because I can assure you that much of what I've gen'd checks those boxes (unless Sorry To Bother You's third-act twist no longer unsettles).
children.textZhHtml:
那么，你是否同意围绕迪士尼对这些工具的锁定进行创造性工作，以产生一些“挑衅性、不舒服、禁忌、明确、颠覆性或挑战规范”的东西，至少会开始接近成为一个至少涉及表面艺术价值的过程？

因为我可以向你保证，我制作的大部分内容都检查了这些框（除非《抱歉打扰》是第三幕转折不再令人不安）。

children.author: jcelerier
children.textRawHtml:
> it's the clash between reality and the intent (whether a location or practical set constraints), the individual skill of everyone involved, costume and prop department

That's not art, that's the limitations that everyone working in this industry have been working night and day to overcome for hundreds of years
children.textZhHtml:
> 这是现实与意图之间的碰撞（无论是取景地还是实际布景的限制），是每一位参与者的个人技艺，以及服装和道具部门

那根本不是艺术，那是这个行业里所有从业者数百年来夜以继日、竭力克服的局限性。

children.author: perching_aix
children.textRawHtml:
A notable chunk of criticism GenAI receives has to do with output irreproducibility and semantic instability relative to the input, so this is a bit entertaining to read.

If there's a way to do something better and worse, then one can absolutely talk about added and absent value. I browse AI generated images a ton, and the difference between beginner / low effort submissions and high effort / advanced submissions is very stark.
children.textZhHtml:
针对生成式AI的批评中，有相当一部分集中在其输出结果不可复现以及语义相对于输入的不稳定性上，因此读起来倒挺有趣的。

既然做一件事有更好和更差之分，那自然就可以探讨附加价值与缺失价值。我平时会浏览大量AI生成的图片，新手或敷衍了事的作品与那些倾注心血、技术精湛的作品之间，差距可谓天壤之别。

children.author: timacles
children.textRawHtml: Yeah one thing people aren’t really grasping with a generative AI is that it fundamentally can’t produce the same thing twice. You can’t really create top notch art with all kinds of inconsistencies
children.textZhHtml: 是的，人们没有真正理解生成式人工智能的一件事是，它从根本上不能产生两次相同的东西。你不可能真正创造出具有各种不一致之处的一流艺术

children.author: perching_aix
children.textRawHtml: Not fundamentally, no. It is entirely possible to keep putting in the same thing, and then getting out the same thing. To be honest, it's outright frustrating that this feature is so often neglected.
children.textZhHtml: 从根本上来说，不是。完全有可能不断放入相同的东西，然后取出相同的东西。老实说，这个功能经常被忽视，这真是令人沮丧。

children.author: simianwords
children.textRawHtml:
I think you are making the same mistake as everyone else with respect to art.

Art has nothing to do with _mechanical_ difficulty. I see this misconception all the time. Examples

- Kumail Nanjiani roided up for his next movie. This has mechanical difficulty, sure. But what does it add to the artistic element?

- Dream Theatre guitarists play olympics with their guitar and play solo's that are mechanically impossible for normal people. Yet we still find Beatles have more artistic value, why?

I hope this popular misconception will die down. I don't want mechanical difficulty in art being praised. I feel these are things people hold on to because mechanical difficulty has some moat and people don't want to give it up.
children.textZhHtml:
我认为你在艺术方面犯了和其他人一样的错误。

艺术与_机械性_难度毫无关系。这种误解我见得太多了。举例

- 库梅尔·南贾尼为下一部电影疯狂增肌。这确实有生理上的难度，但这究竟能为影片的艺术性增添什么呢？

- 梦剧院乐队的吉他手们在吉他演奏上展现出了奥运竞技般的实力，能弹奏出常人根本无法驾驭的超高难度独奏。然而，我们却依然认为披头士乐队具有更高的艺术价值，这是为什么呢？

我希望这种普遍的误解能够早日平息。我不愿看到艺术创作中的机械难度被推崇。我觉得人们之所以对此固守不放，是因为机械难度能构筑起一道护城河，人们不愿放弃这种优势。

children.author: therouwboat
children.textRawHtml:
I don't think that was fair comparison, earlier comment was that creative person can make million dollar art without having million dollar budget, then again creative person can splash paint on canvas while being dead drunk and make most valuable painting.

Most people find it more impressive if you learn to play Dream theater song on guitar than if you program it in daw.
children.textZhHtml:
我认为这样的比较并不公平，之前的评论是创意人士可以在没有数百万美元预算的情况下制作数百万美元的艺术品，然后创意人士可以在喝得酩酊大醉的情况下在画布上泼漆，创作出最有价值的画作。

大多数人觉得如果你学会用吉他演奏梦剧场歌曲，比用DAW编程更令人印象深刻。

children.author: gilleain
children.textRawHtml:
I partly agree with you - I used to think of mechanical difficulty as a kind of 'proof of work' for art. Nowadays I am less interested in the idea of what counts as art or not.

However, consider works like photorealistic drawings of eyes (usually at a very large scale). A lot of people like these, and consider them to be fine art, while others (art critics?) consider them bland. Contrast that with a Picasso flamingo drawn with a single line.

There is artistic value in high-effort, low concept works as well as low-'effort', high-concept works and other combinations. Perhaps what we are all looking for is effort of some kind (I prefer human effort) whether that is conceptual effort or practice in drawing lines on paper.
children.textZhHtml:
我部分同意你的观点 - 我曾经认为机械难度是艺术的一种“工作证明”。如今，我对什么算是艺术或什么不算艺术的想法不太感兴趣。

但是，请考虑诸如逼真的眼睛图画之类的作品（通常尺寸非常大）。很多人喜欢这些，并认为它们是美术，而其他人（艺术评论家？）则认为它们平淡无奇。与毕加索用单线绘制的火烈鸟相比。

高努力、低概念的作品以及低“努力”、高概念的作品和其他组合都具有艺术价值。也许我们都在寻找某种努力（我更喜欢人类的努力），无论是概念上的努力还是在纸上画线的实践。

children.author: simianwords
children.textRawHtml:
I’m curious to understand why you think drawing photorealistic eyes is different from a person beating a Guitar Hero time record.

Both are pushing boundaries in mechanical dexterity. Yet one is considered art and another is not.
children.textZhHtml:
我很好奇，想了解一下为什么你认为画超写实眼睛和某人打破《吉他英雄》的时间记录是两码事。

这两者都在挑战机械灵巧度的极限。然而，一个被视为艺术，另一个却不是。

children.author: gilleain
children.textRawHtml:
If I was feeling unfair I would say there is no difference! However, if a friend drew me an eye on A3 paper would I frame it? Probably yes...

Others have said this better, but art is an qualitative/emotional thing, not a simply measurable quantity of an object. We tend to use the effort put in as a proxy measure for quality, but when photocopiers and 3D printers and - yes - genAI get involved, that becomes harder or impossible.

Beating Guitar Hero is emotional, but not artistic. Photorealistic drawings could be emotional, and may or may not be artistic. Putting a text prompt into a generative model to make a (frankly terrible) music video for $100 is ... something else.
children.textZhHtml:
要是苛刻一点的话，我会说这俩没区别！不过，如果一个朋友在A3纸上给我画了一只眼睛，我会把它裱起来吗？大概会吧……

别人说得比我好，但艺术是一种定性的、情感层面的东西，而不是物体简单的可量化指标。我们往往把投入的精力作为衡量质量的替代指标，但当复印机、3D打印机，还有——没错——生成式AI介入时，这就变得更难甚至不可能了。

打破《吉他英雄》记录能带来情感共鸣，但并不具有艺术性。超写实绘画能触动情感，可能具有艺术性，也可能没有。花100美元把文本提示词输入生成模型，制作一个（坦白说很烂的）音乐视频，那就是……另一回事了。

children.author: actionfromafar
children.textRawHtml:
Anything can be artistic, but some things are very hard to make artistic.

Beating Guitar Hero could artistic. I don't even know how, but I'm sure some artist could beat Guitar Hero in a way that was somehow artistic.

This terrible $100 music video which I'm not even going to watch, is surely not art. Also I'm totally sure some person is going to spend $100 with an AI and make art. It's very hard, but they day will come. The slop-to-art ratio will be astronomical.
children.textZhHtml:
任何事物都可以具有艺术性，但有些事物很难被赋予艺术性。

通关《吉他英雄》或许也能成为一种艺术。我甚至不知道怎么做，但我敢肯定某位艺术家能以某种充满艺术感的方式通关《吉他英雄》。

这个糟糕透顶、我甚至懒得去看的100美元音乐视频，显然不是艺术。同时我也完全确信，会有人花100美元用AI来创作艺术。这非常困难，但那一天终会到来。而粗制滥造之作与艺术品的比例将是天文数字。

children.author: spuz
children.textRawHtml: The problem with this discussion is we are conflating artistic value with economic value. A music video can be valuable as art, but it can also be valuable as a tool for promotion or for generating revenue. If the goal is pure economic efficiency then an AI has the potential to create a music video more efficiently than a human. If the goal is to produce something that makes people feel some kind of emotion then AI will work against you. The same goes for code. I use AI to write code that I'm not precious about but if I want to feel proud of my work, you can be sure I'll write it by hand.
children.textZhHtml: 本次讨论的问题在于我们将艺术价值与经济价值混为一谈。音乐视频可以作为艺术而有价值，但它也可以作为促销或创收的工具而有价值。如果目标是纯粹的经济效率，那么人工智能有可能比人类更有效地制作音乐视频。如果目标是生产出让人们感受到某种情感的东西，那么人工智能就会对你不利。代码也是如此。我使用人工智能来编写我并不珍惜的代码，但如果我想为我的工作感到自豪，你可以确定我会手工编写它。

stories.title: OnePlus halts operations in USA and Europe

comments.author: freedomben
comments.textRawHtml:
OnePlus is one of the saddest stories out there. It was the hacker's choice for a while. It was originally the "Never Settle" phone that ran mostly stock android, had specs maxxed out, price was great, and bootloader was unlocked plus they provided factory images. Those were all reasons I bought a lot of OnePlus phones in the early years.

Then they flushed nearly all of it down the toilet. The day they stopped posting factory images was the day I saw the writing on the wall. Such a shame.
comments.textZhHtml:
OnePlus 是最悲伤的故事之一。 有一段时间，这是黑客的选择。 它最初是一款“永不安定”的手机，主要运行安卓系统，规格最高，价格很棒，引导加载程序已解锁，而且他们还提供了工厂映像。 这些都是我早年购买大量 OnePlus 手机的原因。

然后他们几乎把所有手机都冲进了马桶。 他们停止发布工厂图片的那一天就是我看到不祥之兆的那一天。 真可惜。

children.author: freedomben

Yeah I remember buying a OnePlus 7 Pro with 12 GB (!) of RAM like 7 years ago. The processor was also bleeding edge and that sucker ripped. It combined with stuff like termux was so capable that I used it to run all kinds of stuff that makes little sense on a normal phone. The day that phone retired (from drop damage) was heartbreaking*.

* It actually ran even longer after that as various utilities but not a daily driver, but when I didn't have it with me all the time the convenience slowly waned and it got forgotten
children.textZhHtml:
是的，我记得 7 年前购买了一台配备 12 GB (!) RAM 的 OnePlus 7 Pro。 处理器也很先进，而且那个傻瓜撕裂了。 它与 termux 之类的东西结合起来非常强大，我用它来运行各种在普通手机上几乎没有意义的东西。 手机退役（因跌落损坏）的那一天令人心碎*。

* 在那之后，它实际上运行了更长的时间，作为各种公用事业，但不是日常司机，但当我一直没有带着它时，便利性慢慢减弱，它被遗忘了

children.author: 8n4vidtmkvmk

Good phone. I was worried about that pop up camera failing at some point, but it never did
children.textZhHtml:
这也是我使用的手机。几年前把它给了我的妻子，她一直使用它，直到几个月前它也遭受了致命的下降。非常难过。

children.textZhHtml: 确实，那款弹出式摄像头也曾让我担忧，但从来没出过问题。它经受住了大量口袋棉絮甚至海滩沙子的考验，一直非常可靠。如果质量相当的话，我宁愿用弹出式摄像头来换取目前大多数手机上的“刘海”设计。

children.textZhHtml: 同感。一加证明了弹出式摄像头是前置摄像头的一种完全可行的方案，而不必采用刘海屏。

children.author: xp84
children.textRawHtml:
Boy, do I miss the amount of diversity and innovation we used to have in cell phones. I remember having this phone [1] which put the camera on a little rotating thing so you could use the same one for front or back facing - obviously this either saves money or means they have twice the budget for the cameras as if they had two.

Obviously these cameras themselves were potato cameras compared to modern ones, but this design was still a great innovation and unique. We rarely get many design ideas that are that different today, as everyone mainly just wants to look like an iPhone, and Apple for its part only sits around obsessing on how they can make the iPhone's form more and more featureless and pure.

https://www.mobilecollectors.net/phone/6666/lg-vx-7000
children.textZhHtml:
天哪，我真怀念过去手机那种百花齐放、充满创新的年代。我记得有一款手机[1]，把摄像头装在一个可旋转的小装置上，这样前后置就能共用同一个摄像头——这显然要么省了钱，要么意味着他们给摄像头的预算是装两个摄像头的两倍。

显然，与现代摄像头相比，这些摄像头本身的画质简直惨不忍睹，但这种设计依然是一项伟大的创新，独具匠心。如今我们很少能见到如此截然不同的设计理念了，因为大家主要只想做得像iPhone，而苹果自己则只顾着痴迷于如何让iPhone的外观变得越来越极简和纯粹。

https://www.mobilecollectors.net/phone/6666/lg-vx-7000

children.author: microtonal
children.textRawHtml:
plus has memory compression.

FYI, Android has had zRAM support since KitKat, which is from (checking notes) 2013. Same year as iOS.

iOS uses way less ram than android

Common measurements I have seen is around 40%, I wouldn't say way less, but it is definitely less. Still, 3x more for a model in the same year is impressive (and more than needed to be competitive with iOS) and we should give OnePlus credit for it.

Sadly a lot of low-ish to midrange phones are going back or sticking to 6-8GB today, thanks to the RAM squeeze and the efficiency of iOS is certainly helping Apple here. Certainly nobody is going to complain about the performance of the iPhone 17, despite only having 8GB RAM.

In Android, Samsung doesn't seem to suffer as much. You can pick up an S26 here with 12GB RAM and 256GB storage for 623 Euro, which is a nice midrange price. I guess there are benefits if you can produce your own memory.
children.textZhHtml:
plus 具有内存压缩功能。

仅供参考，Android 从 KitKat 开始就支持 zRAM，这是从（查看笔记）2013 年开始的。与 iOS 同年。

iOS 使用的内存比 Android 少得多

我见过的常见测量值约为 40%，我不会说远少，但它绝对是更少。尽管如此，同年一款机型的容量增加 3 倍仍然令人印象深刻（而且超过了与 iOS 竞争所需的容量），我们应该为此归功于 OnePlus。

遗憾的是，由于 RAM 的挤压，许多中低端到中端手机正在回退或坚持使用 6-8GB 内存，而 iOS 的效率无疑在这方面为苹果提供了帮助。当然，没有人会抱怨 iPhone 17 的性能，尽管它只有 8GB RAM。

在 Android 方面，三星似乎并没有受到太大影响。你可以在这里购买一台配备 12GB RAM 和 256GB 存储空间的 S26，价格为 623 欧元，这是一个不错的中端价格。我想如果你能产生自己的记忆的话会有好处。

children.author: ronbenton
children.textRawHtml: IMO this is the social internet at its best. Pretty obscure question answered relatively quickly with answer and source.
children.textZhHtml: 我认为这是最好的社交互联网。相当晦涩的问题相对较快地得到了答案和来源。

children.author: Waterluvian
children.textRawHtml:
AI immediately gives me the same answer. I can’t tell if I like this easy access to detail or lament the growing irrelevance of “social internet” for these kinds of things.

It reminds me of pre-phone disagreements among pals. You’d argue and argue and maybe eventually agree to disagree. Today someone just looks up the trivia and it’s all over.
children.textZhHtml:
人工智能立即给了我同样的答案。我不知道我是否喜欢这种轻松获取细节的方式，还是感叹“社交互联网”对此类事情越来越不相关。

这让我想起了朋友之间电话前的分歧。你们会争论再争论，也许最终会同意不同意。今天有人随便查一下琐事就完事了。

children.author: pimlottc
children.textRawHtml: Of course, the only reason AI knows about it is because one human posted it online because they wanted to share a good story with another human
children.textZhHtml: 当然，人工智能知道它的唯一原因是一个人将其发布到网上，因为他们想与另一个人分享一个好故事

children.author: justusthane
children.textRawHtml: I really worry about AI leading to the information death of the internet. If everyone is getting their info from AI, rather than actually reading websites/blogs/forums, is there any incentive for posting things like this on the internet in the first place?
children.textZhHtml: 我真的很担心人工智能会导致互联网的信息死亡。如果每个人都从人工智能获取信息，而不是实际阅读网站/博客/论坛，那么首先是否有动力在互联网上发布此类内容？

children.author: bdcravens
children.textRawHtml: I don't know if I'm unique in this, but I find myself asking people I know about things that I know I can easily lookup faster. At this point it's more a social ritual than actual information gathering.
children.textZhHtml: 我不知道我在这方面是否独一无二，但我发现自己向我认识的人询问我知道我可以轻松更快地查找的事情。在这一点上，这更像是一种社交仪式，而不是实际的信息收集。

children.author: order-matters
children.textRawHtml:
same here, but only when it feels like good conversation or is a subject we already discuss. Especially if youre in the same room, then it can become a little trivia game and if no one knows then someone can look it up

related note, my girlfriend is bilingual with spanish, and i only have some old high school classes of spanish to go off of, so whenever she texts me a word i dont recognize i ask her what it means. Aside from helping me understand, she gets a peek into my literacy level (which is admittedly pretty low), i can call out the word when its used again, and i get the impression she likes teaching me these little things.

extending the lesson beyond our little ritual, when you ask another person for the information it goes beyond being useful to each other. it is a bid for connection, and a display that you trust them and their opinion/knowledge on the subject.
children.textZhHtml:
这里也一样，但前提是感觉像是很好的对话或者是我们已经讨论过的主题。 特别是如果你们在同一个房间，那么它可能会变成一个小问答游戏，如果没有人知道，那么有人可以查一下

相关注释，我的女朋友会说西班牙语，而我只有一些旧的高中西班牙语课要上，所以每当她给我发短信时，我不认识一个单词，我会问她这意味着什么。 除了帮助我理解之外，她还了解了我的识字水平（诚然，我的识字水平相当低），当我再次使用这个词时，我可以喊出这个词，而且我觉得她喜欢教我这些小事情。

将课程扩展到我们的小仪式之外，当你向另一个人询问信息时，它不仅仅是对彼此有用。 这是一种建立联系的方式，并表明您信任他们以及他们对该主题的看法/知识。

children.author: seedboot
children.textRawHtml: It's just like we evolved to be social creatures!
children.textZhHtml: 这就像我们进化成为社交生物一样！

children.author: MisterTea

> AI immediately gives me the same answer.

"The search engine found the answer too" is not an interesting response.

“搜索引擎也找到了答案”并不是一个有趣的回答。

children.author: dylan604
children.textRawHtml: In the early days of the smart phone, I had heard it referred to as the Bar Bet Settler 5000. It was pulled out of one's pocket and with its web browser one would use Google's search page to find information to settle the bet. Then, those smart phones got infected with social media apps and the Bar Bet Settler 5000 went the way of the dodo.

comments.author: amccollum
comments.textRawHtml:
My uncle (John Monsour) worked on this movie as the “24 Frame Computer Sync Engineer”. Because film cameras and CRT monitors have different frame rates, you needed to use specialized electronics to synchronize them with the camera frame rate otherwise you would have banding and weird moving artifacts on all the screens. It’s crazy to imagine needing to do this for all the screens visible in these shots.

Later monitor technologies like LCDs don’t have this issue because they don’t have the same moving electron beam illuminating each line of pixels, and it also became cheaper to just replace all the computer screens with CG, so eventually this specialized technical work wasn’t needed anymore, and my uncle ended up doing other things on the movies he worked on.
comments.textZhHtml:
我叔叔（约翰·蒙苏尔）在这部电影中担任“24帧计算机同步工程师”。由于胶片摄影机和CRT显示器的帧率不同，需要使用专门的电子设备将它们与摄影机的帧率同步，否则所有屏幕上都会出现条纹和奇怪的移动伪影。想想这些镜头中所有可见的屏幕都需要这样处理，简直不可思议。

后来的LCD等显示器技术不再有这个问题，因为它们没有那种照亮每一行像素的移动电子束，而且直接用CG替换所有电脑屏幕的成本也变得更低，所以最终不再需要这种专门的技术工作，我叔叔后来在他参与的电影中改做其他工作了。

stories.title: Kaiser nurses say AI, surveillance are making their jobs and patient care worse
stories.titleZh: 凯撒护士称，人工智能与监控正使其工作及患者护理质量恶化。

comments.author: derekdahmer
comments.textRawHtml:
I RFTA and the majority of the complaints are about call center metrics and the pressure to ration care. These are real concerns about misuse of metrics, but not AI. The AI empathy thing was a 2024 pilot that was discontinued.

FWIW my wife works for Kaiser and finds a lot of value in the the medical LLM tools available to her. She tells me being able to do live translation, summarize notes, and quickly get comprehensive answers save her time and help her give better care. Her older patients also frequently come in bringing AI-powered alerts from their apple watches that detected cardiac events.

It's annoying that we use broad terms to describe a set of technologies that in some ways can be problematic and in another ways are very beneficial. We gotta evaluate each of these as they come rather than talk about blanket bans.
comments.textZhHtml:
我的 RFTA 和大多数投诉都是关于呼叫中心指标和配给护理的压力。 这些是对指标滥用的真正担忧，但不是人工智能。 人工智能同理心是一个 2024 年的试点项目，现已停止。

FWIW，我的妻子在 Kaiser 工作，发现她可用的医学 LLM 工具有很大价值。 她告诉我，能够进行实时翻译、总结笔记并快速获得全面的答案，可以节省她的时间并帮助她提供更好的护理。 她的老年患者也经常从他们的苹果手表上带来检测心脏事件的人工智能警报。

令人烦恼的是，我们使用宽泛的术语来描述一组技术，这些技术在某些方面可能存在问题，而在另一些方面却非常有益。 我们必须在每一个问题出现时对其进行评估，而不是谈论全面禁令。

children.author: obscurette
children.textRawHtml:
Being close relative for several med and care workers we have discussed it a lot and consensus is that it really depends. For example relying on LLM summaries sounds great until it doesn't. It doesn't matter whether you misunderstand LLM summary or LLM "misunderstands" you – there are real risks involved, and you wouldn't want them to weigh on your conscience if they were to materialize.

Relying on LLM to summarize things for you has one more issue. To outsiders, this seems like a tedious process, but is actually very important part of the thought process. Wording your thoughts and writing these down helps people to discover new aspects of the problem. It's how people learn.

At the moment consensus is that it must not be banned, but also not mandated in any way - people must take responsibility, and they must be able to decide for themselves where and when the LLM use is justified and where it is not.
children.textZhHtml:
作为几名医务人员和护理人员的近亲，我们对此进行了很多讨论，共识是这确实取决于情况。例如，依赖法学硕士摘要听起来不错，但事实并非如此。无论您误解了 LLM 总结还是 LLM“误解”了您，这都无关紧要 – 都存在真正的风险，而且您不希望它们在发生时影响您的良心。

依靠 LLM 为您总结还有一个问题。在外人看来，这似乎是一个乏味的过程，但实际上是思考过程中非常重要的一部分。表达你的想法并写下来可以帮助人们发现问题的新方面。这就是人们学习的方式。

目前的共识是，它不能被禁止，但也不能以任何方式强制——人们必须承担责任，他们必须能够自己决定在何时何地使用 LLM 是合理的，在哪里是不合理的。

children.author: adrianN
children.textRawHtml: I don’t think that it is possible to both allow the use of LLM and not mandate them in modern metric driven work places. Either you ban them or you force people to use them for game theoretic reasons: they are slower than their peers and quality of the work is harder to measure than quantity. All you achieve is shifting the blame to the employees if the LLM messes up. Come to think of it, that probably is a highly desirable outcome for the decision makers, so perhaps that will actually be the policy that becomes universally adopted.
children.textZhHtml: 我认为在现代指标驱动的工作场所中不可能既允许使用 LLM，又不强制使用它们。要么禁止它们，要么出于博弈论的原因强迫人们使用它们：他们比同行慢，而且工作质量比数量更难衡量。如果法学硕士搞砸了，你所做的就是将责任推给员工。想想看，这对于决策者来说可能是非常理想的结果，所以也许这实际上会成为普遍采用的政策。
